User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Pharma Insights Explorer application.

googleId.description: The Google ID of the user, used for Google login.

registrationDate.description: Date and time the user registered.

MoleculeQuery.title: MoleculeQuery
MoleculeQuery.description: Represents a query for a molecule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the molecule query.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the query. (Relationship: User 1:N MoleculeQuery)

queryText.description: The text of the molecule query.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the query was made.

DiseaseQuery.title: DiseaseQuery
DiseaseQuery.description: Represents a query for a disease.

id.description: Unique identifier for the disease query.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the query. (Relationship: User 1:N DiseaseQuery)

queryText.description: The text of the disease query.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the query was made.

ClinicalTrialData.title: ClinicalTrialData
ClinicalTrialData.description: Represents clinical trial data retrieved from external sources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the clinical trial data.

moleculeQueryId.description: Reference to the MoleculeQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: MoleculeQuery 1:N ClinicalTrialData)

diseaseQueryId.description: Reference to the DiseaseQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: DiseaseQuery 1:N ClinicalTrialData)

source.description: Source of the clinical trial data (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov, WHO ICTRP).

trialId.description: Identifier of the trial in the source database.

summary.description: Brief summary of the clinical trial.

url.description: URL to the clinical trial details.

PatentData.title: PatentData
PatentData.description: Represents patent information retrieved from external sources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the patent data.

moleculeQueryId.description: Reference to the MoleculeQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: MoleculeQuery 1:N PatentData)

diseaseQueryId.description: Reference to the DiseaseQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: DiseaseQuery 1:N PatentData)

source.description: Source of the patent data (e.g., USPTO, PatentsView).

applicationDate.description: Date of application for the patent.

WebIntelligenceData.title: WebIntelligenceData
WebIntelligenceData.description: Represents literature and guideline information gathered from web sources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the web intelligence data.

moleculeQueryId.description: Reference to the MoleculeQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: MoleculeQuery 1:N WebIntelligenceData)

diseaseQueryId.description: Reference to the DiseaseQuery that triggered the retrieval of this data. (Relationship: DiseaseQuery 1:N WebIntelligenceData)

source.description: Source of the data (e.g., PubMed, NCBI).

title.description: Title of the article or guideline.

abstract.description: Abstract or summary of the article or guideline.

url.description: URL to the article or guideline.

publicationDate.description: Publication date of the article or guideline.

InternalKnowledgeDocument.title: InternalKnowledgeDocument
InternalKnowledgeDocument.description: Represents a document uploaded by the user for internal knowledge integration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the internal knowledge document.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the document. (Relationship: User 1:N InternalKnowledgeDocument)

filename.description: Original filename of the uploaded document.

uploadDate.description: Date and time the document was uploaded.

fileLocation.description: Location where the actual document file is stored.

AiInsight.title: AiInsight
AiInsight.description: Represents AI-generated insights and repurposing suggestions for molecules.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AI insight.

moleculeQueryId.description: Reference to the MoleculeQuery that generated this insight. (Relationship: MoleculeQuery 1:N AiInsight)

repurposingScore.description: Repurposing score (0-100) assigned by the AI.

generationDate.description: Date and time the insight was generated.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a generated report compiling data from various agents.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated the report. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

moleculeQueryId.description: Reference to the MoleculeQuery associated with this report. (Relationship: MoleculeQuery 1:N Report)

diseaseQueryId.description: Reference to the DiseaseQuery associated with this report. (Relationship: DiseaseQuery 1:N Report)

reportType.description: Type of the report (e.g., PDF, Excel).

generationDate.description: Date and time the report was generated.

fileLocation.description: Location where the report file is stored.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The 'userId' parameter is the unique identifier for the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for user-related data and segregates data types into separate collections to simplify security rules.

1.  **Users Collection:** Stores user profiles. Authentication relies on `request.auth.uid`, which should match the `userId` in documents to enforce ownership.
2.  **User-Owned Data (MoleculeQuery, DiseaseQuery, InternalKnowledgeDocument, Report):** These entities are stored as subcollections under the `/users/{userId}` path. This establishes clear ownership and allows straightforward security rules based on the `userId`. This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls to verify ownership.
3.  **Data Segregation:** Each entity type (MoleculeQuery, DiseaseQuery, etc.) has its own collection, ensuring a homogeneous security posture within each collection.
4.  **ClinicalTrialData, PatentData, WebIntelligenceData, AiInsight:** These entities are stored as subcollections under their respective query documents. This reflects the relationships between these entities and their parent queries (MoleculeQuery or DiseaseQuery). The `moleculeQueryId` and `diseaseQueryId` fields within these entities link them back to the appropriate parent query.

**Authorization Independence:** Achieved by denormalizing the `userId` into the `MoleculeQuery`, `DiseaseQuery`, `InternalKnowledgeDocument`, and `Report` documents. This removes the need for complex authorization rules that require fetching parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure supports secure `list` operations because each collection has a clear security posture. For example, listing documents in `/users/{userId}/moleculeQueries` is secure because all documents in that collection are owned by the specified user. Path-based ownership ensures that users can only access data under their own user ID, making filtering unnecessary in the security rules.
